entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: A glycophyte grass plant that is sensitive to saline soil profiles.

entities.name: SALT STRESS
entities.description: A physiological constraint caused by high salt levels in soil that affects plant growth and development.

entities.name: TROPICS AND SUBTROPICS
entities.description: Global regions where sugarcane is cultivated in saline soils.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Biological processes in sugarcane that are drastically influenced by salt stress.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC STUDIES
entities.description: Method of identifying salt-tolerant cultivars based on descriptive phenotypes.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Method of identifying salt-tolerant cultivars based on physiological traits.

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Method of identifying salt-tolerant cultivars based on biochemical traits.

entities.name: RELATIVE SALT TOLERANCE INDICES
entities.description: A method (RSTI) used to compare salt tolerance among genotypes across multiple agronomic traits.

entities.name: PROLINE CONTENTS
entities.description: A biochemical trait used as an indicator of salt tolerance in sugarcane cultivars.

entities.name: GENOTYPE RANK
entities.description: A method (GR) used to categorize cultivars as tolerant, moderate, or susceptible.

entities.name: WARD'S MINIMUM VARIANCE
entities.description: A statistical method used to determine the genotype rank of cultivars.

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sugarcane cultivars to grow optimally under salt stress.

entities.name: CO 0239
entities.description: A sugarcane cultivar identified as having low proline contents and being susceptible to salt stress.

entities.name: CO 7717
entities.description: A sugarcane cultivar identified as having high proline contents and being salt tolerant.
